# Torture corpus

The most valuable asset in this repository. Every file here is paired with a
`<name>.expected.json` describing what a correct open produces. **Gate: all files
open without panic and match their expectation, on every PR.**

Contents and rules: [`../../docs/QUALITY.md`](../../docs/QUALITY.md) §1.

Files are small and committed. Multi-GB fixtures are generated by
`glyde-devtools` at CI time and never committed.

**Growth rule:** every `file-wont-open` issue adds its anonymized file here,
in the same PR as the fix.

## Pairing a new case

Add `<name>.<ext>` (or, for a partitioned directory like the Parquet
partition case, a `<name>/` directory) plus `<name>.expected.json` in this
folder. The loader (`crates/glyde-core/tests/support/mod.rs`) pairs both by
stem automatically — no manifest file to update by hand.

## `<name>.expected.json` schema

The canonical schema is the `OpenSummary` struct in
[`../../crates/glyde-core/tests/support/mod.rs`](../../crates/glyde-core/tests/support/mod.rs);
this is a human-readable mirror of it.

```jsonc
{
  // encoding_rs canonical name, e.g. "utf-8", "windows-1252", "utf-16le".
  "encoding": "utf-8",
  // null only for formats without a delimiter concept (e.g. Parquet).
  "delimiter": ",",
  "decimal_separator": ".",
  // Header name of the inferred time column, or null (progressive index).
  "time_column": "timestamp",
  // e.g. "iso8601", "epoch_ms", "excel_serial", "labview_epoch", or null.
  "timestamp_format": "iso8601",
  "row_count": 100,
  "skipped_row_count": 0,
  // One of: "uniform", "segmented_uniform", "irregular", "progressive_index".
  "sampling_class": "uniform",
  "gap_count": 0
}
```

Every field is mandatory in the JSON (no field defaults) so a corpus case
can never assert less than the full inference story.
